title: Register a fishery, cropping water or aquaponic system
body: "This service enables you to apply to the Fish Health Inspectorate to register your fishery, cropping waters, aquaponics unit or to register as a sole transporter, as required under The Aquatic Animal Health (England and Wales) Regulations 2009.  Once you’ve registered, you can apply to the Environment Agency to remove or introduce fish.  You do not need to register rivers and canals.  Before you apply  To register a fishery, cropping water or aquaponic system, you’ll need to provide:   your contact details the address of the site you want to register the size of each site in acres and hectares the names of the species you keep or plan to keep on the site a map marked with the boundaries of the site and names of the surrounding roads   How to mark locations on a map  The map can be:   a scan or photo of a paper map a screenshot from an online map tool such as MAGIC Maps or OS Maps    If you’re using Microsoft Windows 10 or 11, find out how to use the snipping tool to take a screenshot and mark the boundaries of the site.  If you’re using an Apple device, find out how to take a screenshot using an Apple Mac or iPhone.  Apply online  Start now   Other ways to apply  You can also apply by email or post using the RW1 form. Return to the email or postal address within the form.             Register a fishery (email and post version)    PDF, 450 KB, 2 pages     This file may not be suitable for users of assistive technology.    Request an accessible format.    If you use assistive technology (such as a screen reader) and need a version of this document in a more accessible format, please email defra.helpline@defra.gov.uk. Please tell us what format you need. It will help us if you say what assistive technology you use.      Get help  For help using this service email: fish.health.inspectorate@cefas.gov.uk "
